Vietnam

# Vietnam

### A history of resistance

[](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

![Ratnakar Sadasyula's avatar](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!R73I!,w_36,h_36,c_fill,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F81a2c542-8b3f-4ade-98d2-a40950cffbe3_2640x1980.jpeg)

[Ratnakar Sadasyula](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

Nov 17, 2020

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

1

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Share

[Vietnam](https://www.quora.com/Who-has-the-most-feared-military/answer/Ratnakar-Sadasyula-%E0%B0%B0%E0%B0%A4%E0%B1%8D%E0%B0%A8%E0%B0%BE%E0%B0%95%E0%B0%B0%E0%B1%8D-%E0%B0%B8%E0%B0%A6%E0%B0%B8%E0%B1%8D%E0%B0%AF%E0%B1%81%E0%B0%B2) defeated the Mongols, Siamese, French, Americans, Khmer Rouge, Chinese during it’s long history. While their exploits against the French and Americans are quite well known, the foundation was laid during the Mongol invasions, that they managed to counter attack.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!oviu!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Ffccef9bb-7d27-4b9c-bc91-0fa6a4283f93_300x176.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!oviu!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Ffccef9bb-7d27-4b9c-bc91-0fa6a4283f93_300x176.jpeg)

The Mongols and later the Yuan dynasty launched a series of invasions against Vietnam between 1258–88. While they lost in the first campaign, they adopted a scorched earth policy, destroying all farms and starving the invading Mongols of food supplies. They adopted the same tactics during the second invasion, when their capital city Thang Long fell to the Yuan forces. Once again during the 3rd invasion of the Mongols, they beat them back in a series of naval battles, and using the terrain to their advantage. While they later accepted the supremacy of Yuan dynasty to avoid further conflict, they stil managed to retain their independence in taking policy decisions. Exploiting the region’s hilly terrain, jungles, and rivers to it’s advantage, they succesfully ambushed the Yuans in a series of guerilla attacks.

The Tran dynasty that ruled Vietnam in this period used 4 main tactics, that in a way laid the foundation for their military strategy.

**People’s War-**Involving common people in the war, their slogan*Cử quốc nghênh địch( The whole country face to face with the enemy),*and using a spirit of solidarity. The soldiers during the Mongol invasions had two words tattoeed on their arms-Sát Thát( Kill Mongols).

**Scorched Earth policy**- The Tran capital of Thang Long, always fell to the Mongols. But the fact is the Vietnamese knowing they could not protect it, adopted a scorched earth strategy, so that the Mongols were starved of all supplies. Or what they called*Vườn không nhà trống( Both garden and house are empty).*

**Longdays war-**Prolonging the war so that it would extend to the rainy season, during which the Mongols would get bogged down, infected with tropical diseases. As they said*Lấy sức nhàn thắng sức mỏ( "Our troops in good health smite the tired enemy troops).*

Alliance with the neighbouring Champa kingdom, who knew that they could not face the Mongols alone. On top of it having the foresight to prepare for a long war, strengthening their military keeping the food stocks ready. The Mongols on the other hand were not really prepared for a long war of attrition.

Mongols were good at hit and run, the Vietnamese were the masters of this. They let the Mongols move deep into the territory, built secret bases, and ambushed them. Whenever they could not defeat the Mongols, they smartly made a retreat. Yes it was the very same tactics they used against the US Army much later.

The Mongols had a large army, which involved a whole lot of logistics. The Vietnamese understood the importance of attacking the supply lines, and began to target that, sinking their ships, destroying their food stocks, getting them bogged down.

The Vietnamese not just stopped the Mongols, they actually saved South East Asia, unlike Central Asia and China, that were ravaged by the invasions.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!-McA!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fb41299b9-6bf8-430e-8534-6aaa43f1426f_300x182.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!-McA!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fb41299b9-6bf8-430e-8534-6aaa43f1426f_300x182.jpeg)

Again during the crucial battle of Dien Bien Phu during the First Indochina War, the Vietnamese caught the French by surprise, who believing they had no anti aircraft capability, felt they could be easily overrun by aerial bombarding. The Viet Minh however besieged the French, moving massive pieces of artillery, through thick jungles, cuttting tunnels through mountains and then taking a heavy toll of the French supply aircraft using anti aircraft guns. The battle finally caused the French to withdraw and led to Vietnam’s independence too.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!kl49!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fccdbad16-2954-4c3d-89ed-6f3e76e81ea1_250x410.png)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!kl49!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fccdbad16-2954-4c3d-89ed-6f3e76e81ea1_250x410.png)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

While their exploits in the Vietnam War and the network of secret tunnels are well known, not many know about something called the Ho Chi Minh Trail. A logistical network of roads and trails, connecting North and South Vietnam, that provided support in the form of men and material to the Viet Cong, running primarily through Laos. The US National Security Agency called this one of the greatest feats of military engineering as they managed to keep the supply going, in the face of some of the most intense bombing ever.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!4mSR!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F26ec05b4-302b-4118-bccc-54c9f526d121_300x201.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!4mSR!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F26ec05b4-302b-4118-bccc-54c9f526d121_300x201.jpeg)

Finally the Sino-Vietnamese War, the backdrop to that was Vietnam invading Cambodia, and throwing out the murderous Pol Pot regime, ending the genocide there. China which backed the Khmer Rouge, invaded Vietnam in 1979, also alleging mistreatment of it’s ethnic minorities, and occupation of the Spratly Islands. However the Chinese had to withdraw, while Vietnam remained in Cambodia till 1979.

In the course of their long history, Vietnam managed to beat back a whole lot of bigger powers- Mongols, France, US, China using smart tactics and strategy. Making it one of the deadliest armies ever.
